For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

After 34+ clicks with no changes, I need to try clicking on cells I haven't targeted - specifically the white cells at the exact positions in the pattern blocks.
- Plan: Try clicking on the blue cell in the top-right corner that I haven't clicked before. Let me first check the full grid layout.

end of world model.

You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 28 actions; a strong score needs about 12 or fewer. You are 2.3x over the target.
STALL: the board has not changed for your last 9 actions on this level — those actions had no visible effect. Stop repeating the same move; try a different action or target.
REVISIT WASTE: this board state has recurred 9 times recently — you are cycling back through states you have already visited. Break the loop and commit to a new line of play.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the full layout:
- Blue block: rows 0-3, cols 60-63 (4x4)
- Orange block: rows 4-7, cols 60-63 (4x4)
- Bottom bar: row 63, orange cols 0-37, yellow cols 38-63
- Pattern blocks with white/gray/orange cells in the center
The blue block at rows 0-3, cols 60-63 is something I haven't clicked on! Let me try clicking there.
Also, I notice there are 30 objects. Let me understand the structure better:
- ID 0: charcoal background (3460 pixels)
- ID 1: blue 4x4 block (16 pixels)
- ID 2: orange 4x4 block (16 pixels)
- IDs 3-6, 9, 13-16, 20, 25-27: orange 6x6 blocks (36 pixels each) - these are the solid orange blocks
- IDs 7, 11, 17, 19, 24: white blocks of various sizes
- IDs 8, 12, 18, 21, 23: gray blocks of various sizes
- ID 10, 22: orange 2x2 blocks (4 pixels)
- ID 28: orange bar (38 pixels)
- ID 29: yellow bar (26 pixels)
Wait, I see that IDs 3-6 are orange 6x6 blocks (4 blocks), and IDs 9, 13-16 are more orange 6x6 blocks. Let me count:
- IDs 3, 4, 5, 6: 4 orange blocks
- ID 9: 1 orange block
- IDs 13, 14, 15, 16: 4 orange blocks
- ID 20: 1 orange block
- IDs 25, 26, 27: 3 orange blocks
Total: 4 + 1 + 4 + 1 + 3 = 13 orange blocks
But looking at the ASCII, I see 3 columns of orange blocks, each with 5 rows. That's 15 blocks. Hmm, some might be merged with the pattern blocks.
Let me focus on trying the blue block at rows 0-3, cols 60-63. I'll click on row 1, col 61 (center of the blue block).

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: I see the blue block at rows 0-3, cols 60-63 that I haven't clicked. Let me try clicking there.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click on the blue block in the top-right corner
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 1, 'col': 61}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
